name: Bracket tournament (torneo por eliminatorias)
botUpdateServiceIntegrationNotes:
Five separate, independent edits to already-live files (I did not touch any of them myself — bot-update.service.ts, tokens.ts and schema.prisma are all excluded / shared by other in-flight edits):

1. `apps/bot/src/tokens.ts` — append `export const BRACKET_TOURNAMENT_REPOSITORY = Symbol("BRACKET_TOURNAMENT_REPOSITORY");` near the other `*_REPOSITORY` tokens (alphabetical position doesn't matter functionally, just for readability — near `AUTOMATION_REPOSITORY`/`CAPTCHA_REPOSITORY`).

2. `apps/bot/src/bot-update.service.ts` imports — three separate additions, each a single alphabetically-placed line, shown as comments in the snippet:
   - `BracketTournamentRepository` to the first `import type {...} from "@superbot/data"` block (lines 5-63).
   - `InMemoryBracketTournamentRepository` to the second `import {...} from "@superbot/data"` block (lines 64-89).
   - `BRACKET_TOURNAMENT_REPOSITORY` to the `import {...} from "./tokens.js"` block (lines 621-672).

3. Constructor (line ~786-902) — add ONE new param at the very end, using the exact same "@Optional() @Inject(TOKEN) private readonly x: X = new InMemoryX()" pattern as `guardianRepository`/`ownerNetworkRepository`/etc. (the currently-last param). Because it's optional with an in-memory default, this needs ZERO changes to any of the ~35 `new BotUpdateService(...)` call sites in `bot-update.service.test.ts` — the established pattern this repo already uses for every new dependency, per the project memory notes ("patron util para nuevas deps opcionales: va como ULTIMO param, cero churn en los sitios de test").

4. `botHandlers()` array (~line 1116 onward) — add one new entry `{ name: "bracket-tournament.command", handle: ... }`. Placed logically right after the existing `giveaway.join` entry since poll/giveaway/bracket-tournament are all the same category of lightweight community-game commands, but exact position within the array doesn't affect behavior (handlers are tried in order until one returns non-null, and torneo command names never collide with anything else).

5. New private method `handleBracketTournamentCommand` — add it in the same neighborhood as `handlePollCommand`/`handleGiveawayCommand`/`handlePollVote` (~line 7708-7799), which it deliberately mirrors in structure (parse args → validate → call repository → recordAudit → format reply).

Design decisions worth flagging to whoever applies this:
- Both `/torneo_crear` and `/torneo_ganador` require `context.chatId` (group-only, like polls) and `isActorAdmin` (like `/setfeedback`, warn-config, etc.) — recording a fake winner would otherwise let any member corrupt the bracket, so this is gated the same way as other admin-run group features, not left open.
- "One open tournament per chat at a time" is enforced by the BOT HANDLER (checking `getOpenTournament` before `createTournament`), not by the repository itself — same division of responsibility as every other repository in this codebase (poll/giveaway don't self-enforce a "one active X" invariant either).
- No dedicated parser module (like `parsePollCommand`) was added in `modules/games/src/` — the two commands are matched directly off `update.command?.name`/`args`, the same "raw switch on name+args" style already used by `handleUtilityPlusCommand`. This was the simplest option that needed no new files outside what you authorized (packages/data only).
- `/torneo_ganador <entrante>` is a plain re-issuable command (not reply-based, not a stateful "which match is next" flow) — the entrant name alone disambiguates which of the current round's pending matches it resolves, so admins can call it in any order and get clear feedback (`unknown-entrant` lists the still-open pairings) instead of needing to track a match index.
- 5 total command names in play across the whole feature: `/torneo_crear`, `/torneo_ganador` — matches the ask exactly ("a way to record each match's winner... pick the simplest").
risks:
- Typecheck: `node node_modules/typescript/bin/tsc -p packages/data/tsconfig.json --noEmit` is clean for my new file EXCEPT the expected `Property 'bracketTournament' does not exist on type 'PrismaClient'` (5 occurrences, in `PrismaBracketTournamentRepository` only) — this is because the Prisma model doesn't exist in schema.prisma yet and the generated client hasn't been regenerated. This is NOT a bug in my code: the exact same class of error already exists today for every other recently-added sibling repository in this branch (admin-decision-repository.ts, battle-entries-repository.ts, coop-city-repository.ts, eca-rule-repository.ts, known-admin-repository.ts, memory-sequence-repository.ts, quiz-difficulty-repository.ts, speed-game-repository.ts all show the identical pattern) — i.e. this is the established, tolerated state until whoever owns schema.prisma adds the model block and runs `prisma generate`. Once that happens this file needs no further changes.
- `packages/data/src/bracket-tournament-repository.test.ts` — all 8 tests pass via `node node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run packages/data/src/bracket-tournament-repository.test.ts` (tests only exercise `InMemoryBracketTournamentRepository`, so they don't depend on the pending Prisma model at all).
- `PrismaBracketTournamentRepository` itself is untested against a real database (no Postgres available in this environment, consistent with the rest of the repo's Prisma-backed classes) — its logic is identical to the InMemory class (both funnel through the same `resolveWinner`/`firstRound`/`nextRound` pure helpers), so the risk is isolated to the thin Prisma read/write plumbing, not the bracket algorithm.
- I verified `@superbot/module-games` resolves correctly from `packages/data` for BOTH tsc (via the `paths` map in the shared `tsconfig.base.json`, which `packages/data/tsconfig.json` extends) and vitest (via the alias map in the root `vitest.config.ts`) — no new cross-package dependency needed in `packages/data/package.json`.
- I did not add a "confirmation" step before `/torneo_ganador` overwrites a match result, because the interface as designed is not destructive/irreversible in the way a ban/mute is: an admin can correct a mis-typed winner only by... actually there is currently NO way to undo/redo a recorded winner (no `undoWinner` method) — if this is needed later it's a small addition (store the previous patch and expose an `undoLastWinner`), flagging it now since the task said never to weaken a safety gate but this is a brand new feature with no prior gate to weaken; I judged a "no undo" tournament tracker acceptable given it mirrors how a live/real bracket works (once recorded, it stands), but call it out in case the reviewer wants an undo path before shipping.
- Chose "one active tournament per chat" rather than allowing several concurrent brackets — matches the "keep it simple" instruction and every other single-active-thing feature in this codebase (open poll count isn't limited, but giveaways/tournaments-style single-purpose flows in this repo tend to be one-at-a-time); if concurrent tournaments per chat are wanted later, `getOpenTournament`/`createTournament` would need a tournamentId passed through the bot commands instead of always resolving "the" open one.
